claim: Explorer evidence is valid: boundary snapshot implementation is factually supported by project code and tests; assumptions and missing gaps are correctly separated from facts.

evidence.summary: All cited line ranges (62-69, 71-85, 97-109, 100, 104, 111-121, 515-516, 566-572) exist and support the claimed functionality.
evidence.why: Verifier spot-check confirms code references are accurate and implementation matches description.

evidence.summary: All cited test ranges (381-414, 416-452, 454-491, 493-533) exist and cover the claimed scenarios.
evidence.why: Verifier spot-check confirms test coverage matches explorer summary.

evidence.summary: Explorer correctly placed unverified claims (real dirty tree stress test, edge cases, binary file handling) in missing section rather than as facts.
evidence.why: Evidence discipline maintained: no assumptions promoted to facts.

risks: If the missing items (dirty tree stress test, edge cases, binary file handling) contain behavioral surprises, PRD acceptance criteria may not be met in production

result.notes: Explorer evidence is well-structured with specific line numbers and clear separation between verified facts and acknowledged gaps. The implementation claim is supported by actual project code and tests. Missing items are genuine verification gaps that do not invalidate the core implementation fact but represent real-world validation needs.
